message: ci: gate releases on reliability suites (beads_rust-rsei.5.1)

files.patch:
@@ -558,7 +558,7 @@
 {"id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.4","title":"Expand doctor to detect drift, sidecar anomalies, and partial-functionality traps","description":"## Purpose
Make `br doctor` the authoritative workspace inspection tool for drift and partial-breakage states, not just an optimistic health check.

## Scope
- detect DB/JSONL divergence, invalid or contradictory metadata, suspicious sidecar leftovers, and partially functioning workspaces where reads pass but writes fail
- expose actionable structured diagnostics and recommended next steps
- ensure doctor classification aligns with startup and mutation decisions

## Acceptance Criteria
- `doctor` can flag the classes of failures that users are actually hitting in the field.
- A workspace that is unsafe for writes is never reported as simply “OK.”","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:51:51.118030931Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T15:15:35.230897125Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T15:15:35.230587315Z","close_reason":"Doctor workspace health now incorporates drift, sidecar, JSONL, recovery artifact, null-default, and rollback-only write-probe diagnostics instead of reporting only file-state health.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["diagnostics","doctor","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.4","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:56:35.332546461Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.4","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:51:51.118030931Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.4","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:56:44.594245547Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":206,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"The immediate user complaint is exactly this gap: the system can look healthy from one angle and then fail catastrophically on the next write. `doctor` has to become the honest reporter of those partial-functionality traps.","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:59:31Z"},{"id":232,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.4","author":"ubuntu","text":"Progress note: patched `br doctor` to add explicit `db.sidecars` error diagnostics and `db.recovery_artifacts` warnings, plus local sidecar quarantine-in-recovery behavior for `doctor --repair`. Added targeted unit tests and a new E2E case for anomalous WAL-without-SHM repair. Claim is still graph-blocked by beads_rust-rsei.4.1 / beads_rust-rsei.4, so this is progress without status transition until verification finishes.","created_at":"2026-03-12T18:29:52Z"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.5","title":"Fix spurious primary-key errors after successful JSONL-only writes","description":"## Problem
During this planning session, `br create --no-db`, `br comments add --no-db`, and `br dep add --no-db` successfully persisted changes to JSONL and then still returned `DATABASE_ERROR` with `PRIMARY KEY constraint failed`. That makes automation believe the write failed even though state changed underneath it.

## Repro Shape
- create or update beads in `--no-db` mode against the current long-lived workspace
- observe the issue/comment/dependency present afterward
- observe the command exit non-zero with a bogus primary-key error after the successful write

## Acceptance Criteria
- JSONL-only write commands return success when the write succeeded
- duplicate-write protection still works when a write genuinely conflicts
- tests cover issue creation, comment creation, and dependency creation in `--no-db` mode","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-12T04:00:57.722543395Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T06:51:38.339885652Z","closed_at":"2026-03-12T06:51:38.326075963Z","close_reason":"Reduced export_hashes rewrite batching to avoid false PRIMARY KEY failures after successful --no-db JSONL writes, and added storage/e2e regressions that cover create, comments add, and dep add.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["jsonl-only","reliability","write-path"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.5","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T04:00:57.722543395Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":214,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"This bead was discovered while creating the reliability program itself, which is exactly why it belongs here: the planning/tooling path should not be a special case that silently normalizes bad write semantics.","created_at":"2026-03-12T04:01:01Z"},{"id":217,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Root caused the false --no-db PRIMARY KEY failure to the export_hashes rewrite path, reduced the rewrite batch size, and added storage + e2e regressions for create/comments/dep add.","created_at":"2026-03-12T06:51:27Z"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-rsei.5","title":"Install release gates, observability, and operator guidance for reliability","description":"## Why
Reliability work is not complete when the code seems better locally. It is complete when regressions are hard to ship, anomalies are visible, and operators have a playbook for field failures.

## Scope
Turn the new invariants, fixture corpus, realism suites, and recovery paths into mandatory release discipline and operator guidance.

## Deliverables
- CI/release gates for real-world reliability suites
- structured observability around anomaly classification and recovery
- updated disaster-recovery and troubleshooting docs
- a non-hermetic smoke profile that exercises ambient-env and existing-workspace behavior

## Acceptance Criteria
- reliability regressions become release blockers rather than post-release surprises
- operators can diagnose and escalate failures without reverse-engineering the code","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:51:54.326583926Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T03:59:34.054171443Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ops","release-gating","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:51:54.326583926Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:53:18.751657304Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:53:28.736789160Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:53:37.692812501Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":202,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"This bead exists to turn architectural hardening into release discipline; otherwise the new realism work will remain optional and decay over time.","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:58:57Z"},{"id":207,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"This track exists to prevent a repeat of the current situation where the suite is green but the field experience is red. The outcome has to be operational, not just architectural.","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:59:34Z"}]}
-{"id":"beads_rust-rsei.5.1","title":"Make real-world reliability suites mandatory in CI and release verification","description":"## Purpose
Promote the real-world reliability suites from optional or ad hoc checks into mandatory gates for CI, release verification, and high-risk changes.

## Cover
- failure-corpus replay
- crash-injection suites
- long-lived-workspace stress
- doctor/recovery postcondition checks

## Acceptance Criteria
- shipping without these suites requires an explicit conscious override, not omission by default
- release verification documents the outcome of the reliability gates","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:51:57.611605676Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T03:59:37.124139035Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","release-gating","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5.1","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:56:53.826363038Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5.1","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:57:03.126099578Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5.1","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:57:12.811292518Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5.1","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:57:22.300269735Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5.1","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:57:31.628692974Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5.1","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:51:57.611605676Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":208,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"If the new realism suites do not gate merges and releases, they will slowly rot into optional diagnostics and we will be back where we started.","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:59:37Z"}]}

Promote the real-world reliability suites from optional or ad hoc checks into mandatory gates for CI, release verification, and high-risk changes.

## Cover
- failure-corpus replay
- crash-injection suites
- long-lived-workspace stress
- doctor/recovery postcondition checks

## Acceptance Criteria
- shipping without these suites requires an explicit conscious override, not omission by default
- release verification documents the outcome of the reliability gates","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:51:57.611605676Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T16:09:00.414820908Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T16:09:00.414550331Z","close_reason":"Added mandatory CI and release reliability gates with documented emergency override","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","release-gating","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5.1","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:56:53.826363038Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5.1","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:57:03.126099578Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5.1","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:57:12.811292518Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5.1","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:57:22.300269735Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5.1","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:57:31.628692974Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5.1","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:51:57.611605676Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":208,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"If the new realism suites do not gate merges and releases, they will slowly rot into optional diagnostics and we will be back where we started.","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:59:37Z"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-rsei.5.2","title":"Add structured observability and audit records for anomaly and recovery paths","description":"## Purpose
Emit structured logs, audit records, and counters for anomaly classification, quarantine, recovery attempts, and post-recovery validation outcomes.

## Scope
- record what evidence was seen
- record which automatic path ran
- record whether the outcome was success, refusal, or degraded mode
- make the resulting data easy to correlate with operator reports and bug fixtures

## Acceptance Criteria
- field incidents can be mapped back to concrete anomaly and recovery states
- debugging no longer depends on sparse ad hoc stderr snippets","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:52:00.957711972Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T15:32:00.972293878Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T15:32:00.972041656Z","close_reason":"Structured reliability audit records now expose doctor anomaly classification and repair outcomes with tracing and JSON fields.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","observability","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5.2","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:57:41.051238985Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5.2","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:52:00.957711972Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":209,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"A system that recovers or refuses without leaving a clear audit trail is still opaque. Observability is what turns one scary field report into reproducible engineering input.","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:59:40Z"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-rsei.5.3","title":"Rewrite troubleshooting and disaster-recovery playbooks around the hardened model","description":"## Purpose
Rewrite troubleshooting and disaster-recovery docs so they match the hardened product model rather than the current optimistic or partially outdated assumptions.

## Scope
- explain anomaly classes and what they mean
- document quarantine and evidence-preservation behavior
- provide step-by-step operator flows for safe diagnosis, escalation, and recovery
- make it clear when JSONL-only workflows are safe and when they are emergency fallbacks

## Acceptance Criteria
- future operators do not have to rediscover recovery strategy from source code or chat history
- docs match the actual behavior of `doctor`, startup, sync, and repair paths","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:52:04.382375983Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T03:59:43.282354620Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs","operations","recovery"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5.3","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:57:50.583791301Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5.3","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:57:59.792267448Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5.3","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:52:04.382375983Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5.3","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:58:09.055748700Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":210,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"This bead is about preserving hard-won reliability knowledge. If the only accurate runbook lives in engineers’ heads, the next bad incident will still be chaos.","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:59:43Z"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-rsei.5.4","title":"Add a non-hermetic smoke profile for ambient-env and existing-workspace regressions","description":"## Purpose
Add a smoke profile that deliberately runs against pre-existing workspaces under ambient environment conditions instead of the fully scrubbed tempdir harness.

## Scope
- preserve selected ambient env variables that influence routing and output
- exercise commands against existing `.beads` directories rather than only fresh init
- verify that common human/operator invocation patterns do not fail immediately

## Acceptance Criteria
- the project has a lightweight always-run signal for non-hermetic regressions
- bugs caused by environment inheritance or existing-workspace assumptions surface earlier than full incident reports","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:52:07.716630263Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T15:51:54.330743805Z","closed_at":"2026-03-12T15:51:54.327485718Z","close_reason":"Implemented non-hermetic smoke profile and existing-workspace regression coverage","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ambient-env","reliability","smoke"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5.4","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:58:18.430188701Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5.4","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T04:01:06.963277306Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5.4","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:52:07.716630263Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":211,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"The current suite’s strongest blind spot is that it proves behavior in a lab. This smoke profile is meant to be cheap enough to run often while still preserving some of the messiness of real operator environments.","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:59:46Z"},{"id":223,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Starting this now. Planned scope: add a lightweight smoke profile that runs against existing .beads workspaces with selected ambient env preserved, instead of the fully scrubbed tempdir-only harness. I’m wiring it into the existing test/harness surface first so it stays cheap and always-runnable.","created_at":"2026-03-12T15:27:37Z"},{"id":225,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented the non-hermetic smoke coverage. Added a smoke-oriented CLI test runner that preserves a fixed allowlist of routing/output env vars, and added an always-run e2e smoke test against the metadata_custom_paths existing-workspace fixture. Verification: cargo fmt --check passed locally; rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_beads_rust cargo test --test e2e_basic_lifecycle e2e_non_hermetic_smoke_existing_workspace_preserves_env_sensitive_paths -- --nocapture passed; rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_beads_rust cargo check --all-targets passed. Repo-wide clippy still fails on pre-existing tests/common/scenarios.rs pedantic issues unrelated to this bead.","created_at":"2026-03-12T15:51:50Z"}]}

@@ -95,6 +95,54 @@ jobs:
       - name: Run doc tests
         run: cargo test --doc
 
+  reliability-gates:
+    name: Reliability Gates
+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+    timeout-minutes: 60
+    needs: check
+    steps:
+      - uses: actions/checkout@11bd71901bbe5b1630ceea73d27597364c9af683  # v4.2.2
+
+      - name: Install Rust toolchain
+        uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@master
+        with:
+          toolchain: nightly
+
+      - name: Cache cargo
+        uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@e18b497796c12c097a38f9edb9d0641fb99eee32  # v2.9.1
+
+      - name: Failure-corpus replay and doctor/recovery postconditions
+        run: cargo test --test workspace_failure_replay -- --nocapture
+        env:
+          RUST_LOG: beads_rust=debug
+
+      - name: Crash-injection sync matrix
+        run: cargo test --test e2e_sync_failure_injection -- --nocapture
+        env:
+          HARNESS_ARTIFACTS: 1
+          NO_COLOR: 1
+
+      - name: Long-lived single-workspace stress
+        run: cargo test --test e2e_workspace_scenarios scenario_long_lived_single_workspace_stress_suite -- --nocapture
+        env:
+          BR_LONG_STRESS_ITERATIONS: 8
+          HARNESS_ARTIFACTS: 1
+          NO_COLOR: 1
+
+      - name: Concurrent command-family integrity stress
+        run: cargo test --test e2e_concurrency e2e_interleaved_command_families_preserve_workspace_integrity -- --nocapture
+        env:
+          HARNESS_ARTIFACTS: 1
+          NO_COLOR: 1
+
+      - name: Upload reliability artifacts
+        if: always()
+        uses: actions/upload-artifact@ea165f8d65b6e75b540449e92b4886f43607fa02  # v4
+        with:
+          name: reliability-gate-artifacts
+          path: target/test-artifacts/
+          if-no-files-found: ignore
+
   coverage:
     name: Code Coverage
     runs-on: ubuntu-latest

@@ -10,6 +10,15 @@ on:
         description: 'Tag to release (e.g., v0.1.0)'
         required: true
         type: string
+      skip_reliability_gates:
+        description: 'Emergency only: skip reliability gates after recording a reason'
+        required: false
+        type: boolean
+        default: false
+      reliability_override_reason:
+        description: 'Required when skip_reliability_gates is true'
+        required: false
+        type: string
 
 # Ensure only one release runs at a time
 concurrency:
@@ -27,10 +36,6 @@ permissions:
   attestations: write  # Required for artifact attestations
 
 jobs:
-  # Pre-flight: only verify tag matches Cargo.toml version.
-  # Formatting, clippy, and tests are already enforced by ci.yml on push to main.
-  # Duplicating them here caused release failures for cosmetic issues (e.g. cargo fmt
-  # diffs, flaky tests) even when the code was functionally correct.
   preflight:
     name: Pre-flight Checks
     runs-on: ubuntu-latest
@@ -48,11 +53,98 @@ jobs:
           fi
           echo "Version check passed: $CARGO_VERSION"
 
+  reliability-gates:
+    name: Release Reliability Gates
+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+    timeout-minutes: 75
+    needs: preflight
+    steps:
+      - name: Validate reliability override
+        if: ${{ github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' && inputs.skip_reliability_gates }}
+        env:
+          RELIABILITY_OVERRIDE_REASON: ${{ inputs.reliability_override_reason }}
+        run: |
+          set -euo pipefail
+          if [ -z "$RELIABILITY_OVERRIDE_REASON" ]; then
+            echo "::error::reliability_override_reason is required when skip_reliability_gates is true"
+            exit 1
+          fi
+          {
+            echo "## Release Reliability Gates"
+            echo ""
+            echo "Reliability gates were explicitly skipped for this manual release."
+            echo ""
+            echo "Reason: $RELIABILITY_OVERRIDE_REASON"
+          } >> "$GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY"
+
+      - uses: actions/checkout@11bd71901bbe5b1630ceea73d27597364c9af683  # v4.2.2
+        if: ${{ github.event_name != 'workflow_dispatch' || !inputs.skip_reliability_gates }}
+
+      - name: Install Rust toolchain
+        if: ${{ github.event_name != 'workflow_dispatch' || !inputs.skip_reliability_gates }}
+        uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@master
+        with:
+          toolchain: nightly
+
+      - name: Cache cargo
+        if: ${{ github.event_name != 'workflow_dispatch' || !inputs.skip_reliability_gates }}
+        uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@e18b497796c12c097a38f9edb9d0641fb99eee32  # v2.9.1
+
+      - name: Failure-corpus replay and doctor/recovery postconditions
+        if: ${{ github.event_name != 'workflow_dispatch' || !inputs.skip_reliability_gates }}
+        run: cargo test --test workspace_failure_replay -- --nocapture
+        env:
+          RUST_LOG: beads_rust=debug
+
+      - name: Crash-injection sync matrix
+        if: ${{ github.event_name != 'workflow_dispatch' || !inputs.skip_reliability_gates }}
+        run: cargo test --test e2e_sync_failure_injection -- --nocapture
+        env:
+          HARNESS_ARTIFACTS: 1
+          NO_COLOR: 1
+
+      - name: Long-lived single-workspace stress
+        if: ${{ github.event_name != 'workflow_dispatch' || !inputs.skip_reliability_gates }}
+        run: cargo test --test e2e_workspace_scenarios scenario_long_lived_single_workspace_stress_suite -- --nocapture
+        env:
+          BR_LONG_STRESS_ITERATIONS: 8
+          HARNESS_ARTIFACTS: 1
+          NO_COLOR: 1
+
+      - name: Concurrent command-family integrity stress
+        if: ${{ github.event_name != 'workflow_dispatch' || !inputs.skip_reliability_gates }}

 The same records are emitted through `tracing` with target `br::reliability` so field logs can be correlated with doctor JSON, quarantined artifacts, and replay fixtures.
 
+## Reliability Gate Contract
+
+CI and release verification must run these suites before a change can ship:
+
+- `cargo test --test workspace_failure_replay -- --nocapture`
+- `cargo test --test e2e_sync_failure_injection -- --nocapture`
+- `BR_LONG_STRESS_ITERATIONS=8 cargo test --test e2e_workspace_scenarios scenario_long_lived_single_workspace_stress_suite -- --nocapture`
+- `cargo test --test e2e_concurrency e2e_interleaved_command_families_preserve_workspace_integrity -- --nocapture`
+
+These gates cover the failure-corpus replay, crash-injection matrix, long-lived
+workspace stress, concurrent command-family stress, and doctor/recovery
+postconditions. Release builds depend on the gate job by default; the manual
+release workflow exposes an emergency override that requires a written reason
+before artifacts can be built without the gates.
+
 ## Evidence Bundle (Incident Capture)
 
 When a field failure occurs, the following artifacts should be collected for diagnosis:

+
+    log "Reliability gates: crash-injection sync matrix"
+    HARNESS_ARTIFACTS=1 NO_COLOR=1 cargo test --test e2e_sync_failure_injection -- --nocapture
+
+    log "Reliability gates: long-lived single-workspace stress"
+    BR_LONG_STRESS_ITERATIONS="${BR_LONG_STRESS_ITERATIONS:-8}" \
+        HARNESS_ARTIFACTS=1 \
+        NO_COLOR=1 \
+        cargo test --test e2e_workspace_scenarios scenario_long_lived_single_workspace_stress_suite -- --nocapture
+
+    log "Reliability gates: concurrent command-family integrity stress"
+    HARNESS_ARTIFACTS=1 \
+        NO_COLOR=1 \
+        cargo test --test e2e_concurrency e2e_interleaved_command_families_preserve_workspace_integrity -- --nocapture
+}
